reasoning: The threshold for a YES resolution is fixed based on the 360-day average leading up to August 3, 2025. This average is approximately 5.667, meaning each 30-day period must experience 6 or more fatalities to resolve YES. In 2025, Togo has seen a marked escalation in violence. The government confirmed in late July that JNIM militants have killed 54 civilians and 8 soldiers entirely within 2025. Additionally, civil unrest in late June and July led to multiple fatalities during crackdowns (at least 7 to 16 reported by civil society). This indicates an average of ~10+ fatalities per month in 2025, significantly above the 5.667 threshold. Although conflict event fatalities are highly clustered—meaning not every 30-day period will neatly hit the average—the high baseline rate of 2025 makes the probability of >5 fatalities more likely than not. Due to the lack of widely reported major mass-casualty attacks directly in late July, the near-term probability is constrained to roughly 64%. Following the strict instruction that uncertainty must increase (approaching 0.5) over longer horizons, the estimates gently decay for the later resolution dates as potential counter-insurgency successes or negotiated truces become more plausible.
